Surrounded by growth

Nothing dies here

But I keep picking you berries

measly.linePrev: Think of your woman, who’s gone to the west
measly.line: But I’m starving and freezing in my measly old bed!
measly.lineNext: Then I’ll crawl across the salt flats, to stroke your sweet head
stanza: All my bones, they are gone, gone, gone
Take my bones, I don’t need none
Cold, cold cupboard, lord, nothing to chew on!
Suck all day on a cherry stone
Dig a little hole not three inches round —
Spit your pit in a hole in the ground
Weep upon the spot for the starving of me!
Till up grows a fine young cherry tree
When the bough breaks, what’ll you make for me?
A little willow cabin to rest on your knee
What’ll I do with a trinket such as this?
Think of your woman, who’s gone to the west
But I’m starving and freezing in my measly old bed!
Then I’ll crawl across the salt flats, to stroke your sweet head
Come across the desert with no shoes on!
I love you truly
Or I love no-one

mast.line: With four fine masts and lateen sails
stanza: We sailed away on a winter's day
With fate as malleable as clay
But ships are fallible, I say
And the nautical, like all things, fades
And I can recall our caravel:
A little wicker beetle shell
With four fine masts and lateen sails
Its bearings on Cair Paravel
Recall the word you gave:
To count your way across the depths of this arid world
Where you would yoke the waves
And lay a bed of shining pearls!
I dream it every night:
The ringing of the pail
The motes of sand dislodged
The shucking, quick and bright;
The twinned and cast-off shells reveal a single heart of white

melodically.lineNext: I'm ashamed of half the things I say
stanza: 27 ContributorsThe Things I Say LyricsOne of the shortest and most straightforw…
I'm ashamed of half the things I say
I'm ashamed to have turned out this way
And I desire to make amends
But what's it mean
When suddenly we're spent?
Tell me true!
Ambition came and reared its head, and went
Far from you!
Even mollusks have weddings
Though solemn and leaden
But you dirge for the dead
And take no jam on your bread —
Just a supper of salt and a waltz
Through your empty bed
We speak in the store
I'm a sensitive bore
You seem markedly more
And I'm oozing surprise
But it's late in the day
And you're well on your way
What was golden went gray
And I'm suddenly shy
And the gathering floozies
Afford to be choosy
And all sneezing darkly
In the dimming divide
And I have read the right books
To interpret your look
You were knocking me down
With the palm of your eye

The stars are on our fingers

meaningless.line: 2 ContributorsJulia LyricsHalf of what I say is meaningless
meaningless.lineNext: But I say it just to reach you, Julia
stanza: 2 ContributorsJulia LyricsHalf of what I say is meaningless
But I say it just to reach you, Julia
Julia, Julia
Ocean child calls me
So I sing the song of love, Julia
Julia
Seashell eyes, windy smile calls me
So I sing the song of love
Julia
Her hair of floating sky is shimmering
Glimmering in the sun
Julia, Julia
Morning moon touch me
So I sing the song of love
Julia
When I cannot sing my heart
I can only speak my mind
Julia
Julia
Sleeping sand, silent cloud touch me
So I sing the song of love
Julia
Calls me
So I sing the song of love for Julia
Julia, Julia

morphine.linePrev: And left a little shrapnel in his knee
morphine.line: But the morphine eased the pain and the grass grew 'round his brain
morphine.lineNext: And gave him all the confidence he lacked
stanza: Sam Stone came home to his wife and family
After serving in the conflict overseas
And the time that he served had shattered all his nerves
And left a little shrapnel in his knee
But the morphine eased the pain and the grass grew 'round his brain
And gave him all the confidence he lacked
With a Purple Heart and a monkey on his back

morphine.linePrev: Walking in that room when you had tubes in your arms
morphine.line: Those singing morphine alarms out of tune
morphine.lineNext: Kept you sleeping and even, and I didn't believe them
stanza: Walking in that room when you had tubes in your arms
Those singing morphine alarms out of tune
Kept you sleeping and even, and I didn't believe them
When they called you a hurricane thunderclap

morphine.linePrev: You hold on to what you need
stanza: Rifles and rosary beads
You hold on to what you need
Vicodin morphine dreams
Rifles and rosary beads

montgomery.linePrev: This old house would have burnt down a long time ago
montgomery.line: Make me an angel that flies from Montgomery
montgomery.lineNext: Make me a poster of an old rodeo
stanza: Make me an angel that flies from Montgomery
Make me a poster of an old rodeo
Just give me one thing that I can hold on to
To believe in this living is just a hard way to go

muhlenberg.linePrev: So many times that my memories are worn
muhlenberg.line: And Daddy, won't you take me back to Muhlenberg County?
muhlenberg.lineNext: Down by the Green River where Paradise lay
stanza: And Daddy, won't you take me back to Muhlenberg County?
Down by the Green River where Paradise lay
Well, I'm sorry, my son, but you're too late in asking
Mister Peabody's coal train has hauled it away

marriage.linePrev: My traveling companion is nine years old
marriage.line: He is the child of my first marriage
stanza: My traveling companion is nine years old
He is the child of my first marriage
But I've reason to believe
We both will be received in Graceland

marriage.linePrev: Let us say it's for the child
marriage.line: With disagreements about the meaning of a marriage contract
stanza: But now the man and the woman remain in contact
Let us say it's for the child
With disagreements about the meaning of a marriage contract
Conversations hard and wild

marriage.linePrev: Many years later we found ourselves in Canada
marriage.line: Trying to save our marriage and perhaps catch a few fish
stanza: Many years later, I found myself talking to this girl
Who was standing there with her back turned to Lake Marie
The wind was blowing especially through her hair
There was four Italian sausages cooking on the outdoor grill
And they was s-s-sizzlin'
Many years later we found ourselves in Canada
Trying to save our marriage and perhaps catch a few fish
Whatever came first
That night, she fell asleep in my arms
Humming the tune to 'Louie Louie'
Aah baby, we gotta go now

marriage.linePrev: Each empty bottle a dead soldier:
stanza: Mother liked her white wine
She'd have a glass or four
Each empty bottle a dead soldier:
The marriage was the war
When we blurred the edges
When we drank a lot
That's when I got nervous
When the glow got hot
I always get nervous
When the glow gets...
We asked for signs
The signs were sent
The birth betrayed
The marriage spent
Yeah, the widowhood
Of every government
Signs for all to see

madder.lineNext: Throw your hands in the air, say "What does it matter?"
stanza: You can gaze out the window, get mad and get madder
Throw your hands in the air, say "What does it matter?"
But it don't do no good to get angry
So help me, I know

moody.linePrev: It's just as natural as the weather
stanza: There's comfort in melancholy
When there's no need to explain
It's just as natural as the weather
In this moody sky today

marker.linePrev: Between the forceps and the stone
marker.line: Well, I looked at the granite markers
marker.lineNext: Those tributes to finality, to eternity
stanza: Well, I looked at the granite markers
Those tributes to finality, to eternity
And then I looked at myself here
Chicken scratching for my immortality

marker.line: Will the marker stain the skin?
marker.lineNext: Stole the dress I saw you in
stanza: Will the marker stain the skin?
Stole the dress I saw you in
Now nothing comes to mind
Saw a life as override
One more session overdrive
The ceiling is the roof

muscular.linePrev: These are the clouds of Michelangelo
muscular.line: Muscular with gods and sun gold
muscular.lineNext: Shine on your witness in the refuge of the roads
stanza: I pulled off into a forest
Crickets clicking in the ferns

partner.line: Dan + Shay - "Stars"

molly.linePrev: A girl could feel special on any such like"
molly.line: Says James to Red Molly, "My hat's off to you
stanza: Oh, says Red Molly to James, "That's a fine motorbike
A girl could feel special on any such like"
Says James to Red Molly, "My hat's off to you
It's a Vincent Black Lightning, 1952
And I've seen you at the corners and cafés, it seems
Red hair and black leather, my favourite colour scheme"
And he pulled her on behind
And down to Box Hill they did ride

molly.linePrev: Shotgun blast hit his chest, left nothing inside
molly.line: Oh, come down, Red Molly to his dying bedside"
molly.lineNext: When she came to the hospital, there wasn't much left
stanza: "Come down, come down, Red Molly," called Sergeant McRae
"For they've taken young James Adie for armed robbery
Shotgun blast hit his chest, left nothing inside
Oh, come down, Red Molly to his dying bedside"
When she came to the hospital, there wasn't much left
He was running out of road, he was running out of breath
But he smiled to see her cry
And said, "I give you my Vincent to ride"

molly.line: Oh, says Red Molly to James, "That's a fine motorbike
molly.lineNext: A girl could feel special on any such like"
stanza: Oh, says Red Molly to James, "That's a fine motorbike
A girl could feel special on any such like"
Says James to Red Molly, "My hat's off to you
It's a Vincent Black Lightning, 1952
And I've seen you at the corners and cafés, it seems
Red hair and black leather, my favourite colour scheme"
And he pulled her on behind
And down to Box Hill they did ride

molly.linePrev: Then I give you my Vincent to ride"
molly.line: "Come down, come down, Red Molly," called Sergeant McRae
molly.lineNext: "For they've taken young James Adie for armed robbery
stanza: "Come down, come down, Red Molly," called Sergeant McRae
"For they've taken young James Adie for armed robbery
Shotgun blast hit his chest, left nothing inside
Oh, come down, Red Molly to his dying bedside"
When she came to the hospital, there wasn't much left
He was running out of road, he was running out of breath
But he smiled to see her cry
And said, "I give you my Vincent to ride"

molly.linePrev: And down to Box Hill they did ride
molly.line: Oh, says James to Red Molly, "Here's a ring for your right hand
molly.lineNext: But I'll tell you in earnest I'm a dangerous man
stanza: Oh, says James to Red Molly, "Here's a ring for your right hand
But I'll tell you in earnest I'm a dangerous man
For I've fought with the law since I was seventeen
I robbed many a man to get my Vincent machine
Now I'm twenty-one years, I might make twenty-two
And I don't mind dying, but for the love of you
And if fate should break my stride
Then I give you my Vincent to ride"

mario.songTitle: Christmas Card from a Hooker in Minneapolis
mario.linePrev: But someone stole my record player--now, how do you like that?
mario.line: Hey Charlie, I almost went crazy after Mario got busted
mario.lineNext: I went back to Omaha to live with my folks
stanza: Hey Charlie, I almost went crazy after Mario got busted
I went back to Omaha to live with my folks
But everyone I used to know was either dead or in prison
So I came back to Minneapolis, this time I think I'm gonna stay
